missingSurface: Canonical browser-safe live projection channel contract covering Phase 9 operations, assurance, governance, resilience, incident, records, access, compliance, and conformance surfaces.
temporaryFallback: LivePhase9ProjectionGateway exposes read-only safe projection patches only. Missing projection version, trust/runtime binding, graph verdict, or telemetry fence mismatch blocks controls before subscription.
riskIfUnresolved: Browser surfaces could join raw domain events, imply writable controls from stale local state, or blackout unaffected slices when one producer is quarantined.
followUpAction: Replace the local deterministic fixtures with the production event-spine projection service once the backend live channel registry is available, preserving the same schema and fail-closed states.
whyFallbackPreservesAlgorithm: The fallback keeps authority in projection, settlement, graph, runtime, and telemetry contracts; it never treats raw patient, audit, incident, or domain events as browser truth and keeps stale/quarantined behavior slice-bounded.
